To solve this problem, we need to find the maximum element-sum of a complete subset of indices in a 1-indexed array. A subset is complete if the product of every pair of its elements is a perfect square.

### Approach
1. **Understanding Perfect Square Products**: The product of two numbers is a perfect square if and only if the exponents of all prime factors in their prime factorization have the same parity (i.e., both even or both odd). This means that two numbers can be part of the same complete subset if their square-free parts (the product of primes with odd exponents) are the same. The square-free part of a number can be derived by removing all even exponents from its prime factorization.
